dataset: NREL 5MW reference turbine project (QBlade v0.96.2 sample)

pi_items.source: Task description states 'blade length of approximately 10 meters', confirmed in environment README.md which lists task as 'Design 10m HAWT blade with 5+ stations'
Task description states 'at least 5 blade stations', confirmed in environment README.md which lists 'Design 10m HAWT blade with 5+ stations'

Web search confirms NACA 4412 is a real airfoil with 4% camber, 40% location, 12% thickness. Airfoil coordinate file found at /workspace/data/airfoils/naca4412.dat (36 lines, ASCII text with CRLF). File has standard Selig format with header 'NACA 4412' followed by x,y coordinate pairs.

Task description explicitly states 'save the entire project to /home/ga/Documents/projects/hawt_blade.wpa using File > Save Project As'. .wpa is QBlade project format confirmed in environment README and install scripts.

privileged_info_summary: NACA 4412 is a verified real airfoil (4% camber at 40% chord, 12% thickness) with coordinate data present in the environment. The design task requires a 10-meter blade with minimum 5 blade stations using the NACA 4412 airfoil. Output is a QBlade project file (.wpa format) saved as hawt_blade.wpa. The airfoil coordinate data comes from the UIUC airfoil database (confirmed by README mentioning '4 NACA airfoil .dat files from UIUC database'). The NREL 5MW reference turbine sample project exists in QBlade v0.96.2 (downloaded from SourceForge). All metadata claims are grounded in actual data and real turbine design references.
